title: How to remove everything after a character
task: Keep only the part of a cell before a dash, comma, @ or any delimiter.

excel.note: Excel 365: =TEXTBEFORE(A2,"-") is cleaner and handles missing delimiters with a 3rd argument.

libreoffice.note: Identical; TEXTBEFORE works in 24.8+ (with quirks — see its page).

explanation: FIND locates the first dash (position 4) and LEFT keeps everything before it: "ABC-123-XYZ" → "ABC". Note it cuts at the FIRST occurrence; to cut at the LAST one, use the text-after-last-delimiter trick in reverse, and to keep the part AFTER the character swap LEFT for MID. If some cells have no delimiter at all, FIND errors — wrap in IFERROR(...,A2) to pass those through unchanged.
